text.title: Benefits Realization Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding Benefits Realization. Each pairs a binary check and a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Every benefit MUST have a quantified target metric, baseline value, target value, and named business owner BEFORE project approval. Soft benefits without a metric do not count.
rule.rationale: Soft benefits cannot be tracked or disputed. Mandatory quantification at approval time forces honest claims and creates accountability before work begins.

rule.statement: Baseline values MUST be frozen in writing with date and source query before launch. Subsequent measurements compare against the locked baseline; redefinition is forbidden.
rule.rationale: A redefinable baseline destroys measurability. Locking T-0 values keeps the benefit comparable across reporting periods.

rule.statement: Benefit owner MUST be a business stakeholder, NOT the project manager. PM hands off ownership at go-live; orphan benefits decay.
rule.rationale: PMs move to the next project. Adoption and process change — the primary enablers of realization — require an owner who outlasts the PM tenure.

rule.statement: For any benefit where attribution is contested, a control cohort or counterfactual model MUST be pre-registered before launch. Post-hoc attribution is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Post-hoc attribution is unresolvable without a pre-planned counterfactual. Difference-in-differences requires a control group defined before intervention.

rule.statement: Claimable benefits MUST be capped at portfolio level. The same revenue lift cannot be claimed across two concurrent projects in the same portfolio.
rule.rationale: Double-counting inflates portfolio ROI and destroys credibility. CFO/PMO sign-off on benefits above a materiality threshold prevents the failure mode.

rule.statement: If preconditions for Benefits Realization fail (see Skip-If list in AGENTS.md), the operator MUST skip this methodology and document the fallback reason instead of fabricating an output.
rule.rationale: Applying Benefits Realization outside its envelope produces false-precision artefacts. Explicit skip preserves epistemic discipline.
